label: verify-city-of-palmdale-v-palmdale-water-dist
system:
You are the citation verifier in a legal-exploration pipeline. You are given one authority as cited by the pipeline's components, plus snippets showing how it was used. Using web search (prefer primary/official sources: leginfo.legislature.ca.gov, courts.ca.gov, law.justia.com, casetext.com, codes.findlaw.com, fppc.ca.gov), determine:
1. Does the authority exist?
2. Is the citation form correct (reporter cite, year, section number)?
3. Does it plausibly stand for what the snippets use it for?

Statuses: VERIFIED (exists, cite essentially right, usage consistent with what you found); MISCHARACTERIZED (exists but does not support the claimed use, or the cite attaches to the wrong case/section); NOT_FOUND (cannot locate after genuine searching); CONTESTED (real sources point in conflicting directions, or scope is genuinely unsettled); UNCLEAR (could not determine within a reasonable search).

You are NOT determining whether the authority is still good law (no Shepardizing) — say what you actually confirmed, no more. Be strict: a close-but-wrong reporter cite or a holding stretched past what sources show is MISCHARACTERIZED, not VERIFIED.

- Gov. Code §66022

5. Consider migrating toward an individualized water-budget-based tier system tied to parcel-specific characteristics (lot size, occupancy), as upheld in *City of Palmdale v. Palmdale Water Dist.* (2011) 198 Cal.App.4th 926, to strengthen the parcel-attributable-cost nexus.
6. Verify and, if warranted, reallocate recycled-water plant debt service between base rates (systemwide benefit) and the surcharge (peak-d…

[premortem] …behavioral pricing, so the court — following *Capistrano Taxpayers Assn. v. City of San Juan Capistrano* (2015) 235 Cal.App.4th 1493 (verify precise holding and scope) and distinguishing the cost-linked tiers upheld in *City of Palmdale v. Palmdale Water Dist.* (2011) 198 Cal.App.4th 926 (verify) — treated the entire $0.85 as tainted rather than severing a defensible cost-based residue. Counsel's litigation theory ("the incentive language just describes real costs") was not …

## Context

The underlying question is a California public-agency hypothetical: q2 — Drought surcharge funding low-income rebates (Prop 218).

Verify this authority per your instructions.

canonical_cite.description: the correct full citation, if determinable

note.description: 1-3 sentences: what was confirmed or refuted, and the correct cite

text: {"status":"MISCHARACTERIZED","canonical_cite":"City of Palmdale v. Palmdale Water Dist. (2011) 198 Cal.App.4th 926","note":"The case exists and the citation form is correct, but the documents' usage inverts the holding. The Court of Appeal actually struck down Palmdale Water District's water-budget-based tiered rate structure, holding the District failed to carry its Prop. 218 burden of showing that tier pricing corresponded to the actual cost of service (conservation/rate-stability goals alone can't justify disproportionate tiers). The [dialectic] snippet's claim that individualized water-budget tiers were \"upheld\" to \"strengthen the parcel-attributable-cost nexus\" gets the outcome backwards; [premortem]'s parallel characterization (also flagged \"verify\") is likewise wrong, though its hedge at least signals uncertainty. [delphi]'s bare citation without a holding claim is not itself mischaracterized.","source_urls":["https://kmtg.com/news/legal-alerts/water-districts-new-rate-structure-failed-to-comply-with-requirements-of-proposition-218/","https://www.aalrr.com/newsroom-alerts-4059","https://pioneerlawgroup.net/water-district-failed-show-rate-structure-complied-proposition-218-requirements-property-related-fees/"]}

data.canonical_cite: City of Palmdale v. Palmdale Water Dist. (2011) 198 Cal.App.4th 926
data.note: The case exists and the citation form is correct, but the documents' usage inverts the holding. The Court of Appeal actually struck down Palmdale Water District's water-budget-based tiered rate structure, holding the District failed to carry its Prop. 218 burden of showing that tier pricing corresponded to the actual cost of service (conservation/rate-stability goals alone can't justify disproportionate tiers). The [dialectic] snippet's claim that individualized water-budget tiers were "upheld" to "strengthen the parcel-attributable-cost nexus" gets the outcome backwards; [premortem]'s parallel characterization (also flagged "verify") is likewise wrong, though its hedge at least signals uncertainty. [delphi]'s bare citation without a holding claim is not itself mischaracterized.
